messages.content:
Which of the following amino acids provides connection between citric acid cycle and urea cycle?

Select one:

Aspartate

Tyrosine

Lysine

Leucine
Aspartate

The cause of increased ketone bodies in blood in starvation is:


Acceleration of β-oxidation in the liver 


Acceleration of lipolysis in adipose tissue 


Gluconeogenesis acceleration


Decreased insulin/glucagon ratio 


Activation of HMG-CoA synthase in the liver mitochondria
Acceleration of β-oxidation in the liver

Which of the following compounds provides connection between citric acid cycle and urea cycle?

Select one:

Fumarate

Malate

Isocitrate

Citrate
Fumarate

All statements regarding the metabolic changes after three weeks of starvation are true, except:

Select one:

In adipocytes triacylglycerols are actively cleaved to form fatty acids

Enzymes of gluconeogenesis and ketogenesis are active in the liver

Neurons of brain tissue continue to use glucose as a the main source of energy

The level of ketone bodies is almost 100-fold increased, there is a threat for the development of metabolic acidosis
In adipocytes triacylglycerols are actively cleaved to form fatty acids

The main sources of energy for hepatocytes in fasting and starvation are:

Select one:

Glucose

Amino acids

Ketone bodies

Fatty acids
Fatty acids

Which of the following statements describing well fed state is true?

Select one:

Transport of glucose into adipose tissue is reduced

Acetoacetate is the main fuel for the muscles

Amino acids are used for the production of glucose

The main source of energy used by the brain is glucose
The main source of energy used by the brain is glucose

Following 6-hour fasting all the following processes are activated, except:

Select one:

Breakdown of triacylglycerols in adipose tissue

Formation of chylomicrons

Glycogen breakdown in the liver

Gluconeogenesis
Formation of chylomicrons

Choose the correct statement. After overnight fasting:

Select one:

Glycogenesis is stimulated

Glucose level in blood is increased

Enzymes of fatty acid synthesis are activated

Activity of hormone-sensitive lipase is increased
Activity of hormone-sensitive lipase is increased

Glucose is the source of all these substrates for lipogenesis, except:

Select one:

NADPH.Н+

Biotin

Acetyl-CoA

Glycerol-3-phosphate
NADPH.Н+

Choose the main source of energy for red blood cells in fasting and starvation:

Select one:

Fatty acids

Ketone bodies

Glucose

Amino acids
Glucose

For the 1st week of starvation the following points are characteristic:


Hyperlipoproteinemia


Azotemia


Ketonemia 


Hypoglucosemia


Acidosis
Azotemia

Choose the correct statement. After 3 weeks of starvation:

Select one:

Enzymes of gluconeogenesis and ketogenesis are inactivated in the liver

The source of energy in hepatocytes is glucose

The level of ketone bodies is almost 100-fold increased, there is a threat for the development of metabolic acidosis

Neurons use only glucose as asource of energy
The level of ketone bodies is almost 100-fold increased, there is a threat for the development of metabolic acidosis

All following substrates can be used for gluconeogenesis during fasting, except:

Select one:

Glycerol

Arachidonic acid

Alanine

Glutamic acid
Arachidonic acid

In starvation during one week the blood glucose level is maintained due to:


Gluconeogenesis from glycerol 


Glycogen mobilization from the liver


Gluconeogenesis from lactate


Decreased influx of glucose to muscles 


Synthesis of glucose from amino acids
Gluconeogenesis from lactate

All statements regarding organ specialization of metabolism after a meal are true, except:

Select one:

Glucose in red blood cells is oxidized to lactate

The main sources of energy in muscles are fatty acids

Glucose in muscles is used for the formation of glycogen

Ketone bodies are extensively used as a source of energy in the brain
The main sources of energy in muscles are fatty acids

The main source of blood glucose during overnight fasting is:

Select one:

Glycogenolysis in muscles

Gluconeogenesis

Transfer of glucose from the intestine

Glycogenolysis in the liver
Glycogenolysis in the liver

Approximately, 1.5 hours after eating well- balanced food the levels of all following compounds are increased in blood, except:

Select one:

Chylomicrons

Fatty acids

Epinephrine

O Glucose
Epinephrine

Glucose is the source of all these substrates for lipogenesis, except:

Select one:

Glycerol-3-phosphate

O Biotin

O Acetyl-CoA

O NADPH.H+
Biotin

The cause of increased ketone bodies in blood in starvation is:

Acceleration of lipolysis in adipose tissue

☐ Decreased insulin/glucagon ratio

☐ Gluconeogenesis acceleration

Activation of HMG-CoA synthase in the liver mitochondria

☐ Acceleration of ẞ-oxidation in the liver
Activation of HMG-CoA synthase in the liver mitochondria
